philosophy: Deep value, contrarian entry, and downside first
source: Distilled from public Scion letters and Michael Burry interviews. All card language below is paraphrase unless a primary-source quotation is added and verified.
_note: Runtime lens. Cheapness must be paired with hard downside analysis and a catalyst.

master_intro.one_line: Start with downside, buy unpopular value only when hard fundamentals and a catalyst support repricing.
pillars: Use contrarian sentiment as a setup, not proof
master_intro.why_it_matters: This lens tests whether a contrarian position is analytically cheap or merely unpopular.

strength_intro: One thing you did well through this lens:

position_sizing.rule: Size only after quantifying asset coverage, leverage, liquidity, and the permanent-loss scenario.
position_sizing.quote: The downside calculation comes before the upside narrative. (paraphrase)
position_sizing.motive_q: {max_ticker} is {max_pct}% of the portfolio. What hard downside case and balance-sheet evidence justify that size?

averaging_down.rubric_unit: Add only when the margin of safety survives
averaging_down.rule: Add only after updating hard fundamentals, downside, and catalyst probability; a lower market price is not enough.
averaging_down.quote: Cheap can become cheaper when the fundamental downside was misread. (paraphrase)
averaging_down.motive_q: You added to {tickers} while it was losing. What new hard evidence preserved the margin of safety and catalyst?

exit_discipline.rubric_unit: Exit when value is realized or the hard thesis breaks
exit_discipline.rule: Exit when the catalyst occurs, the value gap closes, or hard data invalidates asset value and downside protection.
exit_discipline.quote: The position ends when value is realized or the evidence changes. (paraphrase)
exit_discipline.motive_q: Of the profitable exits, {winner_early}% later rose. Which exits followed value realization, and which lacked an updated hard-value case?

diversification.rubric_unit: Hold a few deeply researched ideas
diversification.rule: Own few positions only when each has deep hard-data research and an independent catalyst; avoid shallow concentration.
diversification.quote: Concentration is earned by depth of work, not by confidence alone. (paraphrase)
diversification.motive_q: You hold {n} instruments and {ai_pct}% shares one driver. Which positions have distinct hard-value evidence and catalysts?

holding_period.rule: Set the horizon from a concrete catalyst and repricing path; do not wait indefinitely for the market to agree.
holding_period.quote: Contrarian patience needs a catalyst and an invalidation point. (paraphrase)
holding_period.motive_q: For {incon_tickers}, did the repricing catalyst move, or did the horizon extend only because the trade stayed underwater?

alpha_beta.rubric_unit: Separate idiosyncratic repricing from factor beta
alpha_beta.rule: Decompose market and value-factor exposure before crediting hard fundamental research.
alpha_beta.quote: A value rally can lift weak and strong theses together. (paraphrase)
alpha_beta.motive_q: You beat the benchmark by {excess}pp with beta {beta}. What came from thesis-specific repricing rather than a value-factor move?

entry_style.rubric_unit: Enter against sentiment only with hard evidence
entry_style.rule: Before entry, record the unpopular consensus, hard valuation evidence, downside, catalyst, and falsifier.
entry_style.quote: Being early or contrarian is useful only when the hard thesis is right. (paraphrase)
entry_style.motive_q: For {entry_ticker}, what hard evidence and catalyst justified going against consensus?
